runs.raw:
Looking at the code, the bug is in the equal case handling (lines 72-75) and the post-processing fix attempt (lines 79-80).

When `a == b`, a union should only include the value once, but the code appends both `a` and `b` (duplicating the value). Then lines 79-80 try to fix this with a hack that only removes the last duplicate if both streams end with the same value - this is clearly wrong as a general fix.

The correct behavior for union when `a == b` is to append the value once and advance both pointers.
